purpose: Define the oracle precedence and divergence classification contract for conformance work so fixture packs, semantic-overlay gates, and user-facing claims classify behavior honestly instead of treating every host-glibc difference as either a failure or support.

authoritative_when: semantic_contract_symbol_join records a documented no-op, fallback, bootstrap-only, unsupported, or abort-only contract
replacement_levels.json declares a narrower replacement claim than a host-glibc parity fixture would imply
oracle_kinds.claim_rule: Authoritative for the current honest claim boundary, but never sufficient for full POSIX/GNU replacement without follow-up fixture evidence.

authoritative_when: FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened intentionally repairs or denies invalid input
strict mode preserves the POSIX/glibc error contract but hardened mode emits a HealingAction
oracle_kinds.claim_rule: Authoritative only for safety_repair classifications; must not be advertised as strict semantic parity.

authoritative_when: the API is standardized and no FrankenLibC-specific documented contract narrows behavior
host glibc behavior is implementation-specific or environment-sensitive
oracle_kinds.claim_rule: Authoritative for normative POSIX semantics, with host glibc used as a compatibility fixture where applicable.

authoritative_when: the ABI wrapper is classified RawSyscall or exposes kernel-specific errno and feature availability
kernel version, seccomp, namespace, or capability state determines the observable result
oracle_kinds.claim_rule: Authoritative for kernel contract and errno shape; host glibc is only an adapter reference.

authoritative_when: the scenario is a GNU/glibc compatibility fixture with no narrower FrankenLibC contract
captured fixtures record deterministic host behavior for the same process environment
oracle_kinds.claim_rule: Authoritative for parity_match fixtures inside their captured environment, not for unsupported or deliberately repaired cases.

authoritative_when: the observed result depends on DNS, NSS, locale database, filesystem state, kernel policy, time, installed helpers, or external services
oracle_kinds.claim_rule: May classify flaky_environment and block claims; it cannot promote a support claim by itself.

required_evidence: strict and hardened modes agree unless hardened safety policy is explicitly involved
fixture or e2e log records expected and actual values
divergence_classifications.user_message: Behavior matched the selected oracle for this scenario and replacement level.

required_evidence: FrankenLibC documented contract names the allowed divergence
negative claim test blocks full parity wording
divergence_classifications.user_message: Behavior intentionally differs under a documented compatibility contract; it is not full semantic parity.

required_evidence: hardened mode emits the expected HealingAction
the repair is deterministic and logged
divergence_classifications.user_message: Hardened mode repaired or denied unsafe input; strict parity and hardened safety are separate claims.

required_evidence: errno or fail-stop result is deterministic
user-facing diagnostic or artifact tells agents not to advertise support
divergence_classifications.user_message: The scenario is intentionally unsupported or platform-gated right now.

required_evidence: the replay command or probe is named
the result does not advance support claims
divergence_classifications.user_message: The result depends on external or host state and must be replayed before making a claim.

required_evidence: missing oracle or fixture is named
owner artifact or bead is named
claim remains blocked even if support_matrix taxonomy says Implemented
divergence_classifications.user_message: The implementation may exist, but the evidence needed for the claim is still missing.

decision_rules.when: semantic_contract_symbol_join lists a blocked semantic contract for the scenario
decision_rules.claim_guard: A host-glibc fixture cannot promote full parity until the documented contract row is retired or reclassified with evidence.

decision_rules.when: runtime_mode=strict and no project-specific contract applies
decision_rules.claim_guard: Strict failures classify as proof_gap or unsupported_contract unless an explicit allowed divergence exists.

decision_rules.when: runtime_mode=hardened and invalid input triggers TSM repair or denial
decision_rules.claim_guard: Safety repair supports hardened safety claims only; it does not advance strict or replacement-level parity.

decision_rules.when: support_matrix status is RawSyscall or behavior is kernel-feature-gated
decision_rules.claim_guard: Kernel unavailable, EPERM, ENOSYS, or namespace-sensitive behavior must be logged before any user-facing support claim.

decision_rules.when: DNS, NSS, locale, filesystem, time, process privileges, or installed helpers affect outcome
decision_rules.claim_guard: Environment evidence can downgrade to flaky_environment or proof_gap, but cannot promote parity by itself.

semantic_class_mappings.required_followup: Add targeted fixtures proving the no-op is either retired or explicitly acceptable for the advertised workload.
Add strict and hardened fixture rows comparing fallback behavior against the chosen normative or host oracle.
Expand fixture and workload evidence beyond bootstrap behavior before advertising replacement readiness.
Keep unsupported/platform-gated behavior documented with deterministic errno, fail-stop, or diagnostic output.
Cover process-exit or fail-stop behavior with isolated subprocess fixtures before compatibility claims mention the surface.

scenarios.description: A standard string operation where host glibc and POSIX-compatible behavior should match in strict mode.
scenarios.expected_claim_effect: supports scoped L0/L1 parity only for this fixture family after logs pass

negative_claim_tests.claim: String fixture parity proves full libc replacement readiness.
Strict-only fixture logs prove both runtime modes.

scenarios.description: Invalid pointer or buffer input where hardened mode may repair or deny while strict mode preserves ABI-compatible error behavior.
scenarios.expected_claim_effect: supports hardened safety claim only when HealingAction and strict-mode behavior are logged separately

negative_claim_tests.claim: A hardened repair is strict glibc parity.
A repaired result without HealingAction evidence proves safety repair.

scenarios.description: A compatibility no-op or deterministic fallback listed in the semantic contract inventory.
scenarios.expected_claim_effect: allows link or bootstrap compatibility wording but blocks full semantic parity wording

negative_claim_tests.claim: support_matrix Implemented means the semantic contract is fully equivalent to glibc.
Allowed divergence can be hidden from users.

scenarios.description: Historical or removed Linux syscall wrappers with deterministic unsupported behavior.
scenarios.expected_claim_effect: blocks support claims while allowing deterministic unsupported errno or fail-stop evidence

negative_claim_tests.claim: A deterministic unsupported errno proves replacement support.
Kernel-gated syscall behavior can be advertised without kernel/env evidence.

scenarios.description: Resolver or NSS behavior that depends on host files, DNS, NSS modules, cache state, or installed helpers.
scenarios.expected_claim_effect: blocks parity claim until environment inputs and replay command are captured

negative_claim_tests.claim: A DNS success on one host proves resolver and NSS replacement support.
Environment-sensitive failure can be closed without replay metadata.

scenarios.description: A bootstrap-only implementation where source and taxonomy exist but evidence is insufficient for semantic replacement claims.
scenarios.expected_claim_effect: blocks claim until the missing fixture, workload, or proof artifact exists

negative_claim_tests.claim: Implemented taxonomy without fixture evidence proves semantic support.
Bootstrap-only behavior proves real workload compatibility.
